message:
refactor(fr-server): move inline parser to lib + add fuzz_inline_parser

Completes the `fr-server` library extraction started in the previous
commit ("expose inline-command parser via library target"): relocates
the full inline-command parser body out of `src/main.rs` into the
library and wires a new cargo-fuzz target directly at the library
entry points.

crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (-161 / +1 net)
  * Removes the in-binary definitions of `InlineParseResult`,
    `should_try_inline_parsing`, `split_inline_args`,
    `try_parse_inline`, and the helper functions they call
    (quote-state machine, terminator detection, etc.). All now
    live in `crates/fr-server/src/lib.rs`.
  * main.rs now imports what it needs from `fr_server::…` at the
    top of the file and otherwise keeps its tcp-server wiring
    unchanged. The `fr-server` binary continues to work
    byte-for-byte — this is a textual relocation, not a semantic
    change.
  * `git diff -w` across the removed lines matches the lib.rs
    additions from the prior commit 1:1.

fuzz/Cargo.toml
  * Add `[dependencies.fr-server]` path dep (the new fuzz target
    pulls `split_inline_args`, `try_parse_inline`, and
    `should_try_inline_parsing` from there).
  * Register `[[bin]] fuzz_inline_parser` pointing at the new
    target.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_inline_parser.rs (new)
  * Structure-aware fuzz input via `arbitrary::Arbitrary`:
      - `FuzzInput::Raw(Vec<u8>)` — slams arbitrary bytes at the
        inline parser to catch panics or state-machine hangs on
        hostile input.
      - `FuzzInput::Structured(StructuredInline)` — builds a
        plausible inline-command line from an
        `InlineArg::{Unquoted,DoubleQuoted,SingleQuoted}` mix,
        plus a `Terminator` discriminant (`\r\n`, `\n`, or none)
        so libfuzzer can mutate each axis independently.
      - `QuotedString { segments: Vec<QuotedSegment> }` models
        escape-sequence + plain-text runs so the fuzzer exercises
        the unescape path at the same rate as the plain-text
        path.
  * Each iteration calls
    `should_try_inline_parsing(first_byte)` → `try_parse_inline`
    → (on success) `split_inline_args`, asserting the parser
    makes progress and returns one of the three documented
    `InlineParseResult` outcomes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -34,6 +34,9 @@ path = "../crates/fr-repl"
 [dependencies.fr-config]
 path = "../crates/fr-config"
 
+[dependencies.fr-server]
+path = "../crates/fr-server"
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resp_parser"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_parser.rs"
@@ -138,3 +141,10 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_rdb_structured.rs"
 test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_inline_parser"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_inline_parser.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
